mechanism: Multilevel statistical modeling with individual-level covariates, country-level contextual variables across survey rounds to estimate relationships between latent constructs. Variance decomposed across individuals nested in country-waves nested in countries, with random intercepts and slopes permitting heterogeneity in effect sizes across groupings.
domain: political science political psychology survey research
distribution: outcome outcome=distrust assumed normal given estimation framework none

mechanism: Task switching paradigm isolates executive control by measuring reaction-time costs for stimulus-response remapping in mixed vs. single-task blocks. Scores calculated as differences in median response latencies, with fixed number of switch trials and random task probabilities on each trial.
distribution: outcome reaction time assumed normal none

mechanism: Probabilistic reasoning task where subjects estimate sequence length from observation of rare vs. common events. Inferences made by comparing mean estimates across groups using t-tests and ANOVAs, with within-subjects and between-subjects factorial designs testing representativeness vs. objective likelihood.

Choice experiments with binary or ternary option sets where dominance relationships create asymmetric preference shifts. Treatment effects estimated via OLS regression and logistic models on binary choice outcomes, with post-choice justification and regret measures as mediators.

Experimental news story with sequential dispatches varying causal information and corrections. OLS regression estimates treatment effects on outcome measures (favorability, likelihood of illegal conduct, belief in investigation cause) with inverse probability weighting and survey weights to account for block randomization.

Factorial experimental design varying stereotype activation (salient vs. not) and participant gender with political knowledge test as outcome. ANCOVA with political interest as covariate tests main effects and interactions across nested groupings (gender, field of study, stereotype condition).
domain: political science stereotype threat experimental psychology
distribution: outcome test scores assumed normal none
